<h2>Supplemental Information: Episode 5</h2>

  <div class="supplement" id="e5.5">
    <h3 class="supplement-title">Texture, Timbre, and Instrumentation in Laura Kaminsky’s <em>As One</em>: A Trans Narrative</h3>
    <h4>Brandon D. Scribner (Florida State University)</h4>
    <h5>Release Date: Thursday, May 21, 2026</h5>
    <div class="floatsupps">
      <div style="float:left; width: 40%;">
        <img class="biopic" alt="Scribner Headshot" src="../supplements/S05Ep05scribner.jpg">

        <p style="clear:both; padding-top: 10px;"><a href="../supplements/S05Ep05Supp_scribner.pdf" target="_blank">Supplemental Materials (PDF)</a>
        </p>

        <p style="font-size: 12px;">

      </div>
      <div style="float:right; width: 60%;">
        <p><strong>Bio:</strong> Brandon Scribner is a PhD student at Florida State University, where they currently teach music theory courses as part of an assistantship. They received a Master’s in Music Theory from Temple University and a Bachelor of Science in Music Education from Towson University. Brandon’s primary research interests include ludomusicology, music theory pedagogy, and functional and modal harmony in popular music. Their most recent projects include developing the “Encounter” topic within the Pokémon video game series, tracing musical narratives in <em>Metaphor: ReFantazio</em>, and a forthcoming podcast episode on gender in Laura Kaminsky’s opera <em>As One</em>.</p>

        <p><strong>Keywords:</strong> <em>As One, Laura Kaminsky, transgender, texture, timbre</em></p>
      </div>
